node: core-and-install (implementer) — includes comprehensive R1 parity repair
non_tdd_reason: prose (base router/adapt markdown), a single concept-array edit in the contract validator + its #274 byte-identical peer, and a shell-script default flip — none has a natural failing-unit-test seam. The R1 parity repair is markdown reconciliation, also no unit-test seam.
regression-green: `node scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` -> "Workflow contract validation passed" (exit 0).
smoke-integration: 3 install.sh cases under scratch HOME (never live config): case1 bare -> enable_adaptive:true; case2 --enable-adaptive=no fresh -> false; case3 stale :true + --enable-adaptive=no -> false, parallel_mode preserved (stale-config trap defeated).
Files (5): commands/workflow-next.md, commands/kaola-workflow-adapt.md, scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js, plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js (byte-identical peer), install.sh.
R1 parity: base commands/workflow-next.md Step 0a-1 reconciled to the gitlab canonical structure; orchestrator-verified normalized diff (glab->gh + path normalization) shows base Step 0a-1 byte-identical to gitlab modulo forge nouns and a single cosmetic line-wrap — full cross-edition parity. Contract tokens (KAOLA_ENABLE_ADAPTIVE, adaptive, fast|full|adaptive, default, typed refusal, design choice) preserved.
Summary: Branch A/B router rewrite (base, gh) now matching the 3 editions; adapt precondition replacement; contract concept array flag-only->default applied identically to the pair; install.sh ENABLE_ADAPTIVE=yes default + config-write runs for both yes/no (RMW preserves parallel_mode), resolution floor unchanged.
